A knowledge-work platform built around GPT-5.4 Pro level intelligence would be really useful. The gap between other models and what Pro can do on complex intellectual work remains stark. I would love to have access in a Codex-like platform with shared file spaces, subagents, etc

I am on my second hour of waiting for Pro results from a two-prompt research question. The results look very promising, but I can't access the sources it is finding easily (a NotebookLM-like interface would help) nor can I inspect intermediate files and code that it created.

students get 28% higher score simply for using pen and paper. it’s crazy. knowledge is not stored linearly in your brain, but as a graph of interconnected concepts. handwriting is more intuitive and engages your brain to absorb information than record it.

it just works.

the better AI models get, the worse "CRITICAL: YOU MUST" prompting works

older models needed loud instructions to pay attention. newer models overtrigger on them - they apply rules too aggressively in contexts where they shouldn’t.

The value produced by models is getting so much better so fast that old hardware is actually getting *more* expensive to rent.

3 years ago, the best model you could run on a H100 chip was GPT-4. 

Now, you can run GPT-5.4 on it, which is smaller and cheaper to run while

Your first app will probably flop. Mine did.
You'll post cringey content that gets 200 views. I did.
You'll hire the wrong developers and lose money. I did.

It's all part of the process.

The app space is saturated is a complete myth.

95% of apps have no idea about distribution.

The barrier to entry to BUILD apps has gone down to zero.

But the barrier to MARKET apps is still high.

If you know how to create viral TikToks, you can dominate.

The building part is
